topic: El Gordo cluster impossibility — verdict reclassification (Not Demonstrated -> Self-Contradicted) per EXP-295
change_summary: Analyst (EXP-295) recommends WIN-054 verdict Not Demonstrated -> Self-Contradicted under sub_rationale category-error import (MOND grouping), plus a tldr_verdict rewrite. Note: queue item routing-note labelled this WIN as Misleading->SC, but the actual current verdict is Not Demonstrated and the EXP-295-P4 patch correctly captures old=Not Demonstrated -> new=Self-Contradicted. Verdict-review items defeat the Step 8a batching gate by definition (criterion 2: verdict change recommended), so this is a singleton-only run.
our_argument_summary: WIN-054 currently sits at verdict Not Demonstrated with finding El Gordo is a genuine 6.2sigma LambdaCDM tension that remains unresolved (Asencio et al. 2023). The dome correctly identifies this real challenge but offers no alternative model: aetheric condensation has no equations, no mass function, no velocity predictions, and no spatial framework for objects at z=0.87. Correctly acknowledging a competitors unresolved problem while providing no dome-specific prediction is Not Demonstrated. The current detail_evidence does contain a structural-incoherence sentence (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: The dome physical universe is capped at disc_radius=20015 km with firmament_height=9086 km — there is no spatial framework in which galaxy clusters at cosmological distances can exist), and contains the silence framing (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: That is not accommodation — it is silence). However the detail_verdict_text explicitly declares Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict verbatim (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_verdict_text), and the inescapable-dilemma framing that the proposed tldr_verdict references is not present in detail_evidence or detail_extra (verified by string search: zero hits for dilemma, inescapable, or either...or in either field). The SC reading is supportable on the structural-foreclosure horn, but EXP-295-P4 as drafted creates a verdict-versus-prose internal contradiction by patching only verdict and tldr_verdict while leaving detail_verdict_text intact.

endorsement.decision: ENDORSE WITH BLOCKING CAVEAT — verdict reclassification is supportable on the structural-foreclosure argument, but the patch as drafted MUST be amended to also update detail_verdict_text, otherwise the WIN ships with verdict=Self-Contradicted while its own detail_verdict_text says Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict verbatim
endorsement.rationale: The SC reading is justified by the existing detail_evidence prose: data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence states there is no spatial framework in which galaxy clusters at cosmological distances can exist — a one-sided structural-foreclosure horn that does match SC pattern (2). The dome bounded-firmament parameters (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km) are explicit and propagate to WIN-029 (already SC, data/wins.json:WIN-029:verdict) and WIN-067 (already SC, data/wins.json:WIN-067:verdict). El Gordo at z=0.87 corresponds to ~3.7e25 m, eighteen orders of magnitude beyond the dome maximum extent; this is structural foreclosure on the dome own terms. HOWEVER, two specific drafting issues block clean integration: (1) detail_verdict_text contains the verbatim line ‘Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict’ (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_verdict_text), which directly contradicts a Self-Contradicted verdict label and which EXP-295-P4 explicitly does NOT patch (per scope_notes.in_scope_for_EXP_295: only verdict + tldr_verdict are patched, detail prose preserved as-is); (2) the new tldr_verdict claims ‘Detail prose already presents this dilemma; verdict now matches’ but the dilemma framing is NOT in detail_evidence or detail_extra — it is the analyst gloss in the EXP narrative and the proposed Part 6.13 subsection only. WIN-047.c3 hole #1 already flagged this for WIN-054 specifically. Both issues are fixable; neither is fatal to the SC reading itself.
endorsement.pattern_match: WIN-054 fits SC pattern (2) by the structural-foreclosure horn (bounded firmament cannot accommodate the WIN cited evidence), similar to WIN-029 (firmament cavity does not produce 7.83 Hz on the dome own equation) and WIN-067 (dome own H(r) implies >=38% Antarctic gravity drop, observed essentially zero). It does NOT match the cleaner numerical-conflict pattern (1) cases like WIN-038 or WIN-053 where the dome own equations produce a contradicting numerical value. The MOND-grouping sub_rationale is well-fit for WIN-054: El Gordo is a canonical modified-gravity-community-cited anomaly (Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023; Banik and Kroupa are well-known MOND advocates), so the import-provenance argument lands cleanly here. The fit is tighter than for WIN-047 (Hubble Law, not MOND-distinctive — flagged in WIN-047.c3 hole #2) and WIN-048 (CMB Axis of Evil, not on McGaugh Triton Station list — flagged in WIN-048.c4 hole #2), and comparable to WIN-052 (RAR is the canonical MOND showcase).

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (not re-examined this cycle — last validated 2026-04-09 cycle 2; no monitoring domain exists for any cosmological WIN)
dome_code_analysis.note: WIN-054 code_analysis tags were validated in c2; this cycle is a verdict-label change, not a code-tag change. Tags below carry forward from c2 unchanged.
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automated calculation. WIN-054 has monitoring=none. No monitor.py domain tracks El Gordo, galaxy clusters, or cosmological structure formation (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_extra: zero of the 39 monitor.py domains track cosmological claims).

Zhang et al. 2015 (V_infall hydrodynamical simulation) — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence cites DOI 10.1088/0004-637X/813/2/129
Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023 ApJ 954 162 (6.2sigma tension) — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence cites DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a
Molnar Birkinshaw 2024 A&A (SIDM resolution exploration) — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence cites DOI 10.1051/0004-6361/202348000
Menanteau et al. 2012 (initial discovery) — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_extra cites DOI 10.1088/0004-637X/753/2/163

dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None at the code level (no automation). At the prose level, the dome accommodation no expansion epoch; aetheric condensation on thermal timescale; high-velocity collisions natural is a bare assertion with no equations or values to compare against (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2. WIN-054 has no monitor.py domain (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_extra: monitor.py 39 domains are all geomagnetic/positional — none track cosmological predictions). Zero automated validation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward from c2. El Gordo is not a standard-physics prediction; it is a measured object from Atacama Cosmology Telescope discovery (Menanteau+ 2012) flagged by Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023 as a 6.2sigma LambdaCDM tension. The dome is not relabeling standard physics; it is hijacking an empirical anomaly that LambdaCDM struggles with.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward from c2. Kim+ 2021 ApJ 923 (mass estimate), Asencio+ 2023 ApJ 954 162 (tension quantification), Menanteau+ 2012 (initial discovery) all predate the dome V51.0 import in late March 2026 by years to a decade. The dome adopted the published El Gordo measurements; no genuine prospective prediction. detail_verdict_text confirms this via the post-hoc test discussion: confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (HST imaging plus Planck SZ catalogue — values reported by Kim et al. 2021).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward from c2. Zero derivation chain from disc_radius=20015 km / firmament_height=9086 km / sun_altitude=5733 km / moon_altitude=2534 km to the El Gordo M_200c=2.13e15 M_sun cluster mass or the V_infall>=2500 km/s collision velocity. The dome accommodation aetheric condensation on thermal timescale is a bare assertion with no equations (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: provides (1) no equations for structure formation or cluster assembly, (2) no cluster mass function, (3) no mechanism, (4) no calculation of expected collision velocities).

holes_found.description: EXP-295-P4 flips verdict to Self-Contradicted but does NOT patch detail_verdict_text, which contains the verbatim line ‘Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict’ (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_verdict_text idx ~700: that the topic is simply absent from the dome physics. Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict: the dome has not demonstrated that its model can form galaxy clusters...). After integration as drafted, WIN-054 will ship with verdict=Self-Contradicted while the prose explicitly declares Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict. This is the textbook verdict-versus-prose mismatch the curmudgeon master rule (Step 3 of curmudgeon.md: Check for verdict mismatches) was written to catch — and it would be created by THIS patch, not absorbed by it. The EXP-295 scope_notes.in_scope_for_EXP_295 says only verdict + tldr_verdict are patched, with detail prose preserved as-is. That scope is too narrow for WIN-054.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider must do ONE of: (a) before integrating EXP-295-P4, add a detail_verdict_text patch that rewrites the offending sentence — minimum: replace ‘Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict: the dome has not demonstrated that its model can form galaxy clusters’ with something like ‘Self-Contradicted is the correct verdict: the dome bounded firmament forecloses the spatial framework in which El Gordo at z=0.87 (~3.7e25 m) can exist, while the WIN cites that very object as confirming evidence’; (b) defer EXP-295-P4 until an analyst pass produces a coordinated detail_verdict_text rewrite alongside the verdict and tldr changes; (c) reject the SC reclassification for WIN-054 specifically and keep it at Not Demonstrated, since the existing detail_verdict_text ND rationale (no equations / no mass function / no mechanism / non-discriminating) is sound on its own and does not need flipping. Option (a) is the cleanest if the analyst can draft the replacement sentence in <10 minutes; (b) is conservative; (c) is the do-no-harm fallback. WIN-052 and WIN-047 do NOT have this problem because their detail_verdict_text does not contain the verbatim ‘X is the correct verdict’ self-anchor.

holes_found.description: The proposed new tldr_verdict reads: ‘El Gordo at z=0.87 is incompatible with a 9086 km firmament; either it exists at the claimed distance (falsifying dome geometry) or it is illusory (in which case its measured properties cannot serve as evidence). Detail prose already presents this dilemma; verdict now matches.’ The closing claim ‘Detail prose already presents this dilemma’ is FALSE on direct read. WIN-054 detail_evidence and detail_extra contain zero instances of dilemma, inescapable, or either...or framing (verified by string search: 0 hits in either field for any of those tokens). The detail prose argues only the structural-foreclosure horn (no spatial framework in which galaxy clusters at cosmological distances can exist; that is not accommodation — it is silence) — it does NOT present the if-illusory horn at all. WIN-047.c3 hole #1 (2026-05-09T14:41Z, dated three hours before the queue push) already flagged this exact issue: ‘On direct read, WIN-054 detail_evidence does NOT structure the contradiction as a dual-horn dilemma...The either/or framing is the analyst gloss in the EXP narrative and the proposed Part 6.13 subsection, not a verbatim claim about WIN-054 existing prose.’ The EXP-295 narrative continued to assert the dilemma framing into the WIN-054 tldr_verdict draft despite this prior flag.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider must rewrite the proposed tldr_verdict closing sentence to drop the false claim about existing prose. Two options: (a) reword to track what the prose actually says — ‘El Gordo at z=0.87 corresponds to a spatial scale ~3.7e25 m, eighteen orders of magnitude beyond the dome 9086 km firmament; the WIN cites that very object as confirming evidence. The dome bounded geometry forecloses the very spatial framework the WIN invokes — verdict now matches the structural-foreclosure argument in the detail prose.’ This is honest about what the prose already says (one-sided foreclosure, not a dual-horn dilemma) and avoids the analyst-gloss inheritance bug. (b) Have the analyst patch detail_evidence or detail_extra to add the dilemma framing explicitly (as a coordinated rewrite of the WIN), so the new tldr can truthfully claim detail prose presents the dilemma. Option (a) is faster; option (b) yields a sharper end product but is out of scope per EXP-295 scope_notes.

holes_found.description: The proposed sub_rationale field is BRAND-NEW and not yet wired into the build pipeline. grep over $CLONE/build.js, $CLONE/build-scripts/, $CLONE/test.js returns zero hits for sub_rationale; node check confirms zero WINs in current data/wins.json carry the field. This is the same finding as WIN-052.c3 hole #1 and WIN-048.c4 hole #5. If decider integrates EXP-295-P4 as drafted, the new sub_rationale value will sit on the JSON object but render nowhere on the live site. The ‘See Part 6.13.’ pointer in sub_rationale_addition (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json:patches[4].sub_rationale_addition) presumes a UI surface that does not yet exist.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider must do ONE of: (a) wire sub_rationale rendering into build-scripts/generate-html.js so the field surfaces in the WIN detail panel near the verdict label, OR (b) downgrade the See Part 6.13. pointer to an audit-trail-only field that is intentionally invisible in the rendered HTML and document the convention in CLAUDE.md, OR (c) defer the patch until the rendering is wired. This is the same recommendation as the parallel WIN-052/WIN-048 reviews — should be resolved once for the whole EXP-295 package, not per-WIN.

holes_found.description: The detail_verdict_text rationale for the current Not Demonstrated verdict is structured around the dome ABSENCE of physics (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_verdict_text: identifying a competitor difficulty is not the same as demonstrating your own model superiority...the dome has not demonstrated that its model can form galaxy clusters, predict their properties, or produce an outcome different from LambdaCDM. Removing the expansion epoch is a necessary but not sufficient condition — you must also show what replaces it. Without that replacement physics, this WIN is non-discriminating). This is a fundamentally different verdict-rationale shape than SC pattern (2): pattern (2) requires that the dome OWN commitments preclude the WIN claim on the dome own terms (e.g., WIN-053 dome own ring-magnet topology produces ~39 nT versus the dome fitted 64852 nT; WIN-067 dome own H(r) predicts >=38% gravity drop at the Antarctic rim, observed zero). For WIN-054, the SC argument is bounded geometry forecloses extragalactic referents, but the existing detail_verdict_text does not run that argument — it runs the absence-of-physics argument. Flipping the verdict label without realigning the verdict-rationale shape leaves the WIN reading as ‘the dome has not demonstrated this’ (Not Demonstrated rhetoric) under a Self-Contradicted label — a defender will spot the rhetorical shape mismatch on close read, even if hole #1 verbatim line is also fixed.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) include in the EXP-295-P4 amendment from hole #1 a fuller detail_verdict_text rewrite that replaces the absence-of-physics argument with the bounded-foreclosure argument as primary, demoting the absence-of-physics critique to a secondary point (no replacement physics either, even granting the dome were positioning galaxies as illusory); or (b) accept that detail_verdict_text remains absence-shaped and that the SC label rests on detail_evidence rather than detail_verdict_text. Option (a) is the consistent fix; option (b) is acceptable if hole #1 is fixed (the verbatim ‘Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict’ line is removed) but leaves a softer rhetorical mismatch.

holes_found.description: The new tldr_verdict drops two critiques the original tldr_verdict carried explicitly: (a) the credit-where-due framing (we do not dispute that El Gordo genuinely challenges LambdaCDM) and (b) the false-dilemma-of-asymmetric-criticism framing (pointing out a problem in one model does not validate a different model that cannot even address the question). Both remain in the detail_verdict_text and so are not lost from the page, but TLDR-only readers will no longer see them. The original tldr_verdict was rhetorically generous-to-the-dome on the El Gordo discovery while still concluding the WIN fails — the new tldr is more confrontational. Defensible (the SC argument is sharper) but worth flagging as a tone shift.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add a final sentence to the new tldr_verdict like ‘The El Gordo discovery itself remains a real LambdaCDM tension (Asencio+ 2023); the failure here is the dome attempt to claim it as evidence for a model that cannot accommodate the spatial scales involved.’ This preserves the credit-where-due framing in TLDR form. Not blocking; the rewrite recommended in hole #2 already addresses some of this.

holes_found.description: Spot-check on routing-note item 2 (Part 6.13 kill-shot tab framing matches our existing kill-shot framing): proposed_subsection_html in EXP-295 references a ‘placement-choice / self-own’ section about the dome having placed cosmological WINs in the strongest-claim category. data/sections.json:part5.html confirms our existing Part 5 framing as ‘six binary tests drawn from the dome Kill-Shot page’ with the 2026-04-17 rule rewrite handled in Section 5.0; nothing in part5.html contradicts the EXP-295 description of how the kill-shot tab is structured. Independent confirmation that WIN-052.c3 hole #4 finding (Part 6.13 framing matches Part 5) replicates for the WIN-054 review pass.
holes_found.recommendation: No action; verification confirms the EXP-295 framing of the kill-shot tab is consistent with our existing Part 5 description.

stronger_arguments.description: The SC reclassification of WIN-054 could be sharpened by foregrounding the dimensional-mismatch numeric in the detail_verdict_text rewrite (hole #1 / hole #4 amendment). El Gordo at z=0.87 has a comoving distance of approximately 2.85 Gpc ~ 8.8e25 m (or angular-diameter distance ~1.5 Gpc ~ 4.6e25 m). The dome maximum extent is firmament_height=9086 km = 9.086e6 m, giving a dimensional ratio of ~10^19 (using angular-diameter distance) or ~10^19 (using comoving). This is a much larger gap than the WIN currently surfaces — the existing detail_evidence says only that there is no spatial framework in which galaxy clusters at cosmological distances can exist without pinning the gap quantitatively. SC pattern (2) cases benefit from a single decisive numeric (compare: WIN-053 ~1660:1 self-contradiction; WIN-067 ~1430x linear-coupling error; WIN-029 ~22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz). Adding ‘a dimensional gap of ~10^19 between the dome 9086 km firmament and El Gordo z=0.87 angular-diameter distance’ to the new detail_verdict_text would give WIN-054 a comparable single-number SC anchor.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence (z=0.87, M_200c~2.13e15 M_sun; firmament_height=9086 km invocation)
data/wins.json:WIN-029:finding (precedent SC numeric anchor: ~22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz)

stronger_arguments.description: The dome accommodation of El Gordo (no expansion epoch; aetheric condensation on thermal timescale; high-velocity collisions natural — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence) actually creates a SECOND structural contradiction available for SC pattern (2): the dome cosmological substitute lambda_A=4283 Mpc (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: an unexplained lambda_A=4283 Mpc redshift scale) is itself a 4283 Mpc = 1.32e23 m parameter, fourteen orders of magnitude LARGER than the dome published disc_radius=20015 km. The dome unified-theory table publishes a cosmological-scale length parameter that exceeds the dome maximum spatial extent by 14 orders of magnitude. This is an INTERNAL contradiction within the dome own published parameter set, not just an external spatial-framework foreclosure. The same lambda_A internal-contradiction was flagged in WIN-052.c3 stronger_arguments[0] for RAR; it is equally available here. Surfacing this in the SC rationale for WIN-054 makes the SC reading stronger because both horns are dome-internal (the dome cannot publish lambda_A=4283 Mpc as a parameter and simultaneously claim disc_radius=20015 km is the spatial bound).

kernel_of_truth.description: El Gordo at z=0.87 IS one of the most striking unresolved tensions for LambdaCDM cosmology. The 6.2sigma significance from Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023 (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: 10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a) is genuine; the V_infall >= 2500 km/s requirement from Zhang+ 2015 hydrodynamics (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: 10.1088/0004-637X/813/2/129) does pose a real challenge for hierarchical structure formation at high redshift; the SIDM resolution attempt (Molnar Birkinshaw 2024 — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence) confirms that mainstream cosmologists treat this as a serious open problem that motivates exotic dark-sector physics. Banik and Kroupa are well-known modified-gravity advocates, so the tension is a legitimate MOND-community-cited anomaly — the import-provenance argument in the EXP-295 sub_rationale lands cleanly. The dome author instinct to cite a real, peer-reviewed LambdaCDM tension rather than fabricate one is the responsible reviewer behavior. NARRATE-CITE: this paragraph cites data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence and data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_extra from this review run.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The El Gordo tension empirical legitimacy depends entirely on the cosmological framework in which it is measured: galaxy-cluster mass and velocity at z=0.87, corresponding to spatial scales ~10^25 m and lookback time ~7 Gyr (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_extra: At z=0.87 lookback time ~7 Gyr it is observed when the universe was ~6.8 Gyr old in LambdaCDM cosmology). The dome published parameters (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km — data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence; data/wins.json:WIN-029) bound spatial extent at ~10^7 m, eighteen orders of magnitude below the smallest scale at which El Gordo is defined. The dome accommodation is a bare assertion with no equations (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence: provides (1) no equations for structure formation, (2) no cluster mass function, (3) no mechanism, (4) no calculation of expected collision velocities) — and the dome only published cosmological parameter, lambda_A=4283 Mpc (data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence), is itself an internal contradiction with the bounded firmament since 4283 Mpc = 1.32e23 m exceeds the dome published 20015 km maximum extent by 14 orders of magnitude. The kernel-of-truth (El Gordo IS a real LambdaCDM tension) does not transfer to the dome because the dome bounded-firmament commitment forecloses the spatial framework in which the tension is measured. NARRATE-CITE: this paragraph cites data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_evidence, data/wins.json:WIN-054:detail_extra, data/wins.json:WIN-029, and monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-052.c3.json from this review run.

package_summary_for_decider: ENDORSE the EXP-295 reclassification of WIN-054 from Not Demonstrated to Self-Contradicted IN PRINCIPLE (the SC reading is supportable on the bounded-firmament-forecloses-galaxy-clusters horn, and the MOND-grouping sub_rationale is the second-tightest fit of the five-WIN package after WIN-052), BUT do NOT integrate EXP-295-P4 as drafted. Two BLOCKING amendments required before integration: (1) detail_verdict_text contains the verbatim line ‘Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict’ which directly contradicts a Self-Contradicted verdict label and which EXP-295-P4 does not patch — decider must add a detail_verdict_text patch alongside, OR defer EXP-295-P4 pending analyst rewrite, OR reject the SC reclassification for WIN-054 specifically; (2) the new tldr_verdict claims ‘Detail prose already presents this dilemma’ but the dilemma framing is NOT in WIN-054 detail_evidence or detail_extra — verified by zero string-search hits for dilemma/inescapable/either-or; this echoes WIN-047.c3 hole #1 from earlier today which already flagged the same false-attribution-to-prose issue — decider must rewrite the closing sentence of the new tldr to match what the prose actually says (one-sided structural foreclosure, not dual-horn dilemma). One moderate finding inherited from WIN-052/WIN-048: sub_rationale field not yet wired into build pipeline. One additional moderate: the existing detail_verdict_text rationale is absence-of-physics shaped (Not Demonstrated rhetoric) while SC pattern (2) wants foreclosure-by-own-commitments shaped — fix in same amendment as hole #1 if amendment is taken. Two stronger-argument suggestions: foreground a single decisive dimensional gap numeric (~10^19 between firmament 9086 km and El Gordo angular-diameter distance ~1.5 Gpc) to give WIN-054 an SC anchor comparable to WIN-053 1660:1 / WIN-067 1430x; surface the lambda_A=4283 Mpc internal contradiction as a SECOND SC horn (both horns dome-internal). Net: the SC reading is sound; the patch as drafted is not. Decider should either coordinate the missing detail_verdict_text patch with the analyst (preferred — single coherent rewrite), or hold WIN-054 in the queue while WIN-047/048/052/055 proceed. Do not auto-pop this item until the verbatim ‘Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict’ line is addressed.
deeper_analysis: WIN-054 is a harder SC reclassification case than WIN-052 (which had both horns of the dilemma verbatim in its prose) or WIN-048 (which already invoked self-contradiction with WIN-034 inline). The SC argument for WIN-054 rests on a single horn (bounded firmament forecloses galaxy clusters at cosmological distances) that IS in the existing detail_evidence, but the verdict-shape rhetoric in detail_verdict_text was written for the Not Demonstrated verdict and has not been touched by EXP-295. From a defender perspective, the strongest pushback against the WIN-054 SC reclassification specifically would be: (1) ‘the dome is silent on extragalactic ontology rather than denying it’ — addressed in EXP-295 anticipated_objection c, with the lambda_A=4283 Mpc parameter constituting a positive cosmological-scale claim that breaks the silence reading; (2) ‘the WIN existing detail_verdict_text says Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict — flipping the label without patching the rationale is goalpost-moving in plain sight’ — this is hole #1 of this review and is the strongest defender argument; the curmudgeon counter-argument c in EXP-295 (revision-visibility cost) acknowledges the goalpost-moving risk but does not address it for the case where the WIN own prose self-anchors to the prior verdict; (3) ‘the dilemma framing in the new tldr is novel construction not present in detail prose’ — this is hole #2 of this review and was already flagged in WIN-047.c3 three hours before the queue push. The package_summary_for_decider above closes both gaps: (a) hole #1 must be addressed by amending EXP-295-P4 to include detail_verdict_text or by deferring; (b) hole #2 must be addressed by rewriting the tldr closing sentence. With both addressed, the SC reclassification is on solid ground. Without them, integrating EXP-295-P4 as drafted produces a verdict-versus-prose internal contradiction that is itself a curmudgeon-grade finding the next review cycle would catch. Net recommendation: ENDORSE the SC reclassification; BLOCK integration of the patch as drafted; require coordinated detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict amendments before WIN-054 specifically lands.
